authoritative_sources.title: Marketing Compliance - Frequently Asked Questions
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official SEC staff guidance for the investment adviser marketing rule, authoritative for extracted performance, hypothetical performance, gross-versus-net presentation, and disclosure boundaries.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains the lane to stop at disclosure-risk handling when audience-facing performance claims are not supported by the required gross-net, benchmark, or approval context.

authoritative_sources.title: Investment Company Reporting Modernization Rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official SEC guidance describing Form N-PORT reporting content, T+1 reporting basis, liquidity categories, derivatives disclosures, and portfolio-level reporting artifacts used in asset-management analytics workflows.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Defines the capital-markets records and reporting semantics that distinguish the lane from generic analytics, especially for funds, holdings, liquidity, and exposure packages.

authoritative_sources.title: SEC Extends Effective and Compliance Dates for Amendments to Investment Company Reporting Requirements
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official SEC notice stating the current compliance dates for the 2024 Form N-PORT amendments, authoritative for avoiding assumptions that the amended reporting cadence is already in force.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Keeps the package aligned to the currently effective public baseline and prevents it from overclaiming requirements that remain delayed or under further SEC review.

authoritative_sources.title: TRACE Reporting and Dissemination
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official FINRA workflow reference describing time-of-execution reporting windows, dissemination timing, and security-type-specific reporting semantics for fixed-income markets.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains fixed-income scorecards so they do not treat OMS timestamps, dissemination timestamps, and time of execution as interchangeable facts.

authoritative_sources.title: GIPS Standards for Firms
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized industry standard for fair presentation, composite construction, required report contents, and performance-calculation discipline across asset-management reporting.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Defines realistic composite, benchmark, and performance-report language so the lane stays grounded in capital-markets reporting rather than generic dashboard phrasing.

authoritative_sources.title: GIPS Guidance Statement on Benchmarks for Firms
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized public guidance directly addressing benchmark selection, custom benchmarks, benchmark changes, and benchmark-related disclosures in performance reporting.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains benchmark-relative analytics so the lane records benchmark changes and custom blends explicitly instead of silently swapping benchmark baselines.

authoritative_sources.title: Principles for Financial Benchmarks
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized benchmark-governance standard covering data sufficiency, data-input hierarchy, transparency, methodology changes, audit trail, and accountability mechanisms.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Escalation for stale, conflicting, or incomplete data
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains the lane to escalate when benchmark methodology, input hierarchy, or benchmark change records are not explicit enough to support confident reporting.

authoritative_sources.title: FINRA Rule 4511 - General Requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official FINRA books-and-records rule governing how regulated firms make and preserve records relied on by capital-markets analytics and reporting workflows.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains outputs so they require preserved, retrievable records rather than narrative claims about supposed performance or execution quality.

authoritative_sources.title: Electronic Storage of Broker-Dealer Records
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official SEC interpretive source explaining Rules 17a-3 and 17a-4 record sets, including blotters, ledgers, order tickets, confirmations, and securities records used as analytics evidence.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Defines the named records the lane must request before asserting metric support, disclosure readiness, or fixed-income execution conclusions.

workflow_stages.name: Request framing and source validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate the reporting audience, benchmark context, reporting window, currency basis, composite or account scope, and the systems that establish the initial truth set before analysis begins.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Metric definition and benchmark selection
workflow_stages.description: Define metric logic, denominators, benchmark methodology, composite or share-class scope, event-date rules, and source hierarchy without collapsing capital-markets distinctions.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Analysis or reporting package assembly
workflow_stages.description: Assemble performance, attribution, exposure, liquidity, or execution-quality packages with explicit metric logic, refresh assumptions, audience framing, and caveats.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Narrative synthesis or disclosure review
workflow_stages.description: Translate analytical outputs into commentary or disclosure-support language while preserving gross-net, extracted-performance, benchmark, and approval boundaries.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Escalation for stale, conflicting, or incomplete data
workflow_stages.description: Stop or downgrade outputs when benchmark governance, price hierarchy, holdings data, execution timestamps, or system reconciliation conflicts could change the answer materially.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: trade blotters, execution fills, allocations, and order or ticket records
positions, holdings, cash, price-source files, corporate-action records, and benchmark constituent histories
IBOR, ABOR, fund-accounting, risk-engine, and performance-engine extracts
benchmark methodology files, benchmark-change notices, custom-blend definitions, and exception logs
performance presentations, client reports, marketing decks, commentary drafts, and approval-history artifacts
valuation hierarchy records, market-data vendor extracts, TRACE data or equivalent transparency inputs, and reconciliation notes
systems_of_record: order management systems and execution management systems
investment book of record and accounting book of record platforms
fund accounting, general ledger, and cash platforms
client reporting, marketing review, case-management, and document-management systems

allowed: Define metrics, benchmark baselines, source hierarchy, refresh assumptions, and caveat structure for capital-markets analytics requests.
Produce evidence-backed analytical briefs, metric-definition summaries, reconciliation notes, and disclosure-risk escalation outputs tied to named records.
Normalize capital-markets terminology and preserve source lineage without implying portfolio, valuation, or approval authority.
escalate: Required tenant facts, benchmark methodology, valuation hierarchy, currency policy, or disclosure audience materially change the metric outcome.
Portfolio, trading, valuation, legal, compliance, or publication-approval authority changes the safe reporting posture.
Different systems, timestamps, benchmark records, or price sources produce unreconciled outcomes for the same reporting context.
refuse: Requests for investment recommendations, execution instructions, valuation or NAV signoff, legal interpretation, GIPS verification, or final disclosure approval.
Requests for stronger causal, forecast, benchmark, or override claims than the records support.
Publication or disclosure requests where records are incomplete, stale, or conflicting enough to change the conclusion.

domain_failure_modes: Using IBOR, ABOR, fund-accounting, and risk-engine values interchangeably without an explicit source hierarchy.
Treating benchmark-relative return, total return, price return, and currency-adjusted return as if they were the same metric.
Publishing analytics from stale benchmark constituents, unresolved corporate actions, or incomplete price-source coverage.
Collapsing OMS timestamps, TRACE dissemination timestamps, and time of execution into one execution-quality figure.
Presenting extracted, hypothetical, or custom-benchmark performance without the disclosure support required by the governing public baseline.
Crossing from descriptive analytics into portfolio decisions, valuation signoff, legal interpretation, or final marketing approval.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Metric definition is ambiguous or disputed
Source data is incomplete, stale, or conflicting
Stakeholder asks for a claim or disclosure not supported by the data
Different systems disagree on the metric outcome
A stakeholder asks for stronger conclusions than the data supports
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Broker-dealer versus investment-adviser evidence and disclosure posture
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Capital-markets tenants may operate under broker-dealer, investment-adviser, fund, or hybrid obligations that change which records and approvals govern publication.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant operating model, regulated entity type, and approver map before final disclosure-support outputs are treated as publishable.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Current baseline for the delayed 2024 Form N-PORT amendments
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: The SEC extended the 2024 amendment compliance dates in 2025 and continues to review further changes, so field-level expectations may differ from the currently effective public baseline.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Use the currently effective pre-amendment baseline unless the tenant explicitly supplies an amended reporting schema and compliance posture.

unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Benchmark changes, custom blends, vendor prices, evaluated prices, and internal valuation adjustments may materially alter the reported metric.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant benchmark-governance and valuation-hierarchy policy before publishing benchmark-relative or valuation-sensitive analytics.
